categories.summary: The channel discussion centered on ElizaOS development updates and community management. The most significant technical announcement came from odilitime, who reported that elizaOS and DegenAI were added as default payment methods in elizaOS for x402 routes. This represents a concrete integration milestone for the payment infrastructure. Shaw provided critical project updates, explaining that ElizaCloud has been consolidated into the main eliza repository at github.com/elizaOS/eliza. The team migrated away from Vercel hosting and implemented support for cheaper container solutions to reduce operational costs. Shaw clarified the project structure, emphasizing that elizaOS operates as a fair launch without VC backing or traditional team structure, consisting instead of builders who use and develop the technology. The platform enables developers to build and monetize their own applications on top of the infrastructure. An inquiry about ElizaCloud-specific communication channels was addressed, with Shaw directing users to the consolidated GitHub repository. The discussion also included community management aspects, with Shaw setting clear expectations about the founder's role focusing on product development rather than token price management, and warning against negative commentary. The technical focus remained on infrastructure improvements, cost optimization, and payment system integration.

faqs.question: Is there any specific separate chat or news stream / update log specifically for the ElizaCloud?

helpInteractions.context: User asking if there were problems with ElizaCloud
helpInteractions.resolution: Checked in to diagnose potential issues
Directed to consolidated GitHub repository at github.com/elizaOS/eliza and explained migration from Vercel with container cost optimizations

actionItems.description: Completed integration of elizaOS and DegenAI as default payment methods for x402 routes
Migrate ElizaCloud from Vercel to cheaper container infrastructure
Consolidate ElizaCloud documentation into main eliza repository at github.com/elizaOS/eliza

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains three brief inquiries with no substantive technical discussions or resolutions. guru0 asked about cloud hosting costs for running Eliza as a Twitter bot and whether it's feasible on Mac mini hardware, but received no response. tomy.0315 posted a general developer availability message without specific context. binarycookies inquired about Hyperfy connectivity status for elizaos, also without receiving an answer. No technical solutions, implementations, or decisions were documented in this segment. The chat represents unanswered questions rather than active problem-solving or development discussions.

faqs.question: What's expected costs of running Eliza on cloud for a twitter bot? and is it even possible on a Mac mini or not?
Does elizaos still connect to Hyperfy?

categories.summary: The discussion channel had minimal technical activity. The primary technical question involved Twitter API integration, specifically whether the X API is required or if the login method still works. The user guru0 expressed concern about API costs for posting. odilitime responded that this question was answered in the coders channel. stan0473 flagged an issue with the elizaos.github.io/summary/day page being stuck since May 4th, requesting assistance from a specific user. odilitime speculated this might be related to their GitHub account configuration. The rest of the chat consisted of promotional content for voting and a brief bullish sentiment comment.

faqs.question: Do you need X api for twitter now or does login method still work?

helpInteractions.context: Question about Twitter API requirements and login method
helpInteractions.resolution: Directed to answer in coders channel

actionItems.description: Fix elizaos.github.io/summary/day page stuck since May 4th, investigate GitHub account configuration

categories.summary: The discussion centered on a missing Hyperfy plugin for ElizaOS. binarycookies discovered the plugin had been removed from GitHub despite seeing it days earlier, noting version mismatches between elizaos core and the plugin. odilitime confirmed the plugin needed updates and suggested using a coding agent to fix compatibility issues. binarycookies had previously downloaded the starter pack and planned to attempt repairs. odilitime provided a GitHub link to the eliza-3d-hyperfy-starter plugin, though it returned a 404 error. As a workaround, odilitime directly sent binarycookies a zip file of the plugin via DM. A separate discussion addressed Twitter API requirements, with guru0 asking about authentication methods and API costs. odilitime clarified that X API is now required but noted recent pricing revisions made it more affordable. da4tner offered assistance to binarycookies during troubleshooting. itssowenn4462 introduced themselves as an AI Full Stack Engineer with 8+ years experience in AI agents, automation workflows, and multimodal AI, expressing interest in connecting with builders.

faqs.question: Do you need X api for twitter now or does login method still work?

helpInteractions.context: Missing Hyperfy plugin from GitHub that was needed for ElizaOS integration
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided GitHub link initially, then sent zip file directly via DM when link showed 404 error

helpInteractions.context: Offered assistance while binarycookies was troubleshooting the missing plugin issue
helpInteractions.resolution: Offer made but binarycookies resolved issue with odilitime's help

actionItems.description: Update Hyperfy plugin to match current elizaos core version
Fix compatibility issues in Hyperfy plugin using coding agent
Repair downloaded Hyperfy plugin from starter pack

categories.summary: This Discord segment contains minimal substantive technical discussion. The primary content consists of self-promotional messages and general greetings rather than collaborative problem-solving. harry346165 shared details of a completed client project featuring a modern web stack (Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind, Shadcn, TanStack Query, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Redis) deployed on AWS with Docker-Kubernetes orchestration. The AI implementation utilized LangGraph and CrewAI agents powered by Claude 3.5 and GPT-4o, incorporating RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) over Pinecone and Weaviate vector databases. The developer indicated capability with blockchain integration and PlayStation ecosystem work. cryptologos_00014 posted market analysis regarding ElizaOS coin, noting positive net fund flow despite declining prices, suggesting potential market manipulation. Two users (xwasi and radu83._49711) posted brief introductions seeking collaboration opportunities, with radu83._49711 mentioning experience building autonomous blockchain agents using FastAPI and Next.js. magicyte shared an external link without context. No technical discussions, debugging sessions, or collaborative problem-solving occurred in this segment.

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains no technical discussions, decisions, or problem-solving activities. The conversation consists entirely of casual greetings and social interactions among community members. Users exchanged various forms of greetings including 'hi', 'hey', 'GM' (good morning), and 'hello'. One user (mdmnvest) shared a Mother's Day greeting. Another user (rsn6958) posted a self-introduction describing their background in AI and full-stack development, mentioning their focus on practical solutions, clean code, and production-ready systems, though this was not part of any ongoing technical discussion. No concrete technical solutions, implementations, code discussions, or problem-solving occurred during this chat segment. The channel appears to have been used primarily for social interaction during this time period rather than technical collaboration or development work.

The discussion focused on the operational costs of running an Eliza Twitter bot. odilitime provided cost estimates indicating the monthly expense was previously around $100/mo but has decreased to approximately $10/mo. The actual cost varies significantly based on configuration options and usage patterns, particularly the volume of replies the bot generates. A brief security concern was raised by gokumaster64 questioning if something was compromised, though this was not elaborated upon or resolved in the visible conversation.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to run an Eliza as a Twitter bot every day?

helpInteractions.context: Needed information about operational costs for running an Eliza Twitter bot
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided cost breakdown showing it was $100/mo previously, now around $10/mo, with costs varying based on configuration and reply volume

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment consists primarily of introductory messages and self-promotional posts from various AI and full-stack engineers. The channel shows minimal technical discussion or problem-solving activity. Multiple users (a.debbie, valleybeyond7991, lambyone) posted brief greetings. The bulk of content came from engineers advertising their services: _ky0078 specializes in autonomous agents using LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, and multimodal AI including voice assistants and trading bots; harry346165 focuses on Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript stack combined with AI tools like LangChain and LangGraph; fabio7311 emphasizes 10+ years in ML with expertise in GANs, SOMs, DBNs, PINNs, and SNNs for production systems; trace.g highlighted project management approach for LLM systems and RAG implementations. One notable non-technical discussion occurred when .chomppp raised concerns about ElizaOS token support and Shaw removing ElizaOS from his X bio, though this received no response. satsbased made brief comments about favorable market conditions and needing a catalyst. No concrete technical problems were solved, no architectural decisions were made, and no implementation details were discussed. The segment represents a networking/introduction phase rather than active development collaboration.

faqs.question: Is the team still supporting the ElizaOS token, or has it been abandoned? I also noticed that Shaw removed ElizaOS from his X bio.

categories.summary: A developer (rma_bot) reached out to the coders channel seeking permission to test a multi-agent orchestrator built in Python using claude-agent-sdk. The system includes an A2A-protocol read-only research agent with integrations for Tavily, fetch, DuckDuckGo, and arXiv via MCP (Model Context Protocol). The agent is designed to operate in a sandbox channel with restricted permissions: read-only access, no direct messages, and only responds to @-mentions. The developer had previously contacted inquiries@elizalabs.ai and was looking for the appropriate contact regarding OAuth invite/whitelist procedures. They expressed willingness to port the solution to Eliza if required by the community. odilitime responded offering to handle the OAuth setup via direct message, providing the needed administrative support for testing the agent on the server.

faqs.question: Who can I DM about OAuth-invite/whitelist for testing my multi-agent orchestrator?

helpInteractions.context: rma_bot needed OAuth access to test a multi-agent orchestrator on the server and was unsure who to contact for whitelist/invite permissions
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime offered to handle the OAuth setup via direct message

actionItems.description: Set up OAuth invite/whitelist for rma_bot's multi-agent orchestrator testing in sandbox channel
Evaluate potential porting of Python-based multi-agent orchestrator (with Tavily/fetch/DuckDuckGo/arXiv MCP integrations) to Eliza framework

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains minimal substantive technical discussion. The conversation consists primarily of introductory messages and casual greetings. User rsn6958 posted a self-introduction describing their background in AI and full-stack development, emphasizing practical problem-solving, clean code practices, and production-ready systems. The message highlights focus areas including efficiency, accuracy, user experience, maintainability, and security. User brickolascage made a brief comment about someone's computer skills, though the context is unclear. User dondada0420 greeted the channel and inquired about chatters. No technical problems were solved, no architectural decisions were made, and no concrete implementations were discussed. The segment lacks meaningful technical discourse, code reviews, debugging sessions, or collaborative problem-solving that would typically characterize active development discussions.

The main technical discussion centered on v2 registry infrastructure for third-party Eliza plugins. thegreatluna8713 reported that plugin-undesirables@2.0.3 is ready for submission to the v2 registry but encountered 404 errors when attempting to access the elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai. They have generated JSON metadata prepared and noted their plugin uses BUSL-1.1 licensing, preventing direct PR to the monorepo. odilitime acknowledged the issue and indicated they would investigate, suggesting it might be accidental. They also mentioned a potential policy change to revert back to PRs directly to elizaOS/eliza repository. The conversation was interrupted by security concerns when shrektwo noticed suspicious activity, identifying compromised admin accounts. odilitime confirmed ongoing scammer issues in the Discord. The technical resolution for the registry submission process remains pending investigation.

faqs.question: I've got a third-party plugin (plugin-undesirables@2.0.3) ready for the v2 registry. The target elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai are returning 404s. Is the v2 registry infra still private pending the 2.0.0-beta launch? Should I hold my PR until the repo goes public?

helpInteractions.context: Unable to access v2 registry infrastructure (404 errors on elizaos-plugins/registry and plugins.elizacloud.ai) for third-party plugin submission
helpInteractions.resolution: Acknowledged the issue and committed to investigating; suggested potential policy change to revert to PRs to elizaOS/eliza

actionItems.description: Investigate 404 errors on elizaos-plugins/registry repo and plugins.elizacloud.ai for v2 registry access
Determine final submission process for v2 third-party plugins and communicate to plugin developers
Address Discord security issues with compromised admin accounts
